arguments.system:
You are a world-class front-end designer creating a single self-contained HTML file for a LiveTile Design presentation system. NO external dependencies -- all CSS and JS must be inline. The design should be a dark-themed, enterprise-grade adaptive card/slide viewer.

DESIGN REQUIREMENTS:
1. LAYOUT: A 12-column x 7-row CSS Grid canvas at 16:9 aspect ratio (1280x720 viewport). The canvas is the centerpiece.
2. THEME SYSTEM: Include at least 4 dark themes with smooth transitions. Each theme defines: bg, card, accent, accentLight, accentGlow, border, text, textMid, textSubtle colors. Use CSS custom properties for all colors.
3. TEMPLATE BROWSER: A horizontal scrolling strip of template thumbnails (at least 6 templates). Clicking one loads that layout into the canvas.
4. TILE TYPES: Each tile is a card on the grid. Include these tile types with REAL rendered content (not placeholders):
   - Hero tile (full-width header with title, subtitle, badge)
   - KPI tiles (large number, label, delta indicator with up/down arrow)
   - Bar Chart (SVG-based vertical bars with axis labels, gridlines, and animation)
   - Pie/Donut Chart (SVG with segments, labels, center stat)
   - Line Chart (SVG polyline with data points, gradient fill under curve)
   - Grouped Column Chart (multiple series side by side per category)
   - Radar/Spider Chart (SVG polygon overlays on a radial grid)
   - Stacked Bar Chart (horizontal or vertical with segment breakdown)
   - Data Table (striped rows, header highlighting, sortable-look)
   - Pipeline/Flow (horizontal steps with connector arrows and status)
   - Status Grid (colored indicator dots/blocks with labels)
   - Stat Block (icon + big number + label + spark trend)
5. TEMPLATES: Define at least 6 complete slide layouts:
   - Title Overview (hero + workflow cards + metadata)
   - Dashboard (header + 4 KPIs + bar chart + pie chart)
   - Analytics (header + line chart + radar + KPIs)
   - Agent Analytics (agent-themed KPIs, session metrics, CSAT, resolution rates)
   - Channel Analytics (grouped column for multi-channel volume + trend line)
   - Executive Brief (fact sheet + emphasis blocks + comparison)
6. ANIMATIONS: Tiles should have a subtle entrance animation (stagger by grid position). Charts should animate their data on load (bars growing, lines drawing, pie segments expanding).
7. TILE FLIP: Clicking a tile flips it 180deg to show a JSON profile inspector on the back.
8. JSON INSPECTOR: A right-side panel showing the full slide profile as syntax-highlighted, collapsible JSON.
9. HEADER: App title "LiveTile Design Studio" with a version tag and theme switcher row.
10. FOOTER: Tile count, theme count, template count stats.

VISUAL POLISH:
- Glass-morphism card effects (backdrop-filter blur, translucent backgrounds)
- Accent glow borders on hover
- Gradient mesh background on the canvas
- Smooth 300ms transitions everywhere
- Typography: Use system font stack with clear hierarchy (32px hero, 14px body, 11px labels)
- All charts must use actual SVG rendering with real data points, not images or placeholders
- Color palette should feel premium, cinematic, dark -- like a Bloomberg terminal meets Apple keynote

SAMPLE DATA: Use realistic enterprise agent/bot analytics data:
- KPIs: "Active Sessions: 1,247 (+142)", "Resolution Rate: 87.3% (+2.1%)", "Avg Response Time: 1.4s (-0.3s)", "CSAT Score: 4.6 (+0.2)"
- Charts: Weekly channel volumes (Teams, Web Chat, Email), quality trends, evaluation scores
- Tables: Agent performance rankings with scores, dates, status badges

OUTPUT: Return ONLY the complete HTML file content. No markdown fences. No explanation. Just the raw HTML from <!DOCTYPE html> to </html>. The file must work when opened directly in a browser with zero dependencies.
arguments.prompt: Create the single-file HTML LiveTile Design Studio presentation as specified in the system prompt. Make it visually stunning, technically impressive, and production-quality. Every chart must render with real SVG graphics, every animation must be smooth, and the overall feel should be cinematic and elegant. Push the boundaries of what a single HTML file can achieve.
